name: pre-dispatch agent config — orchestrator must run bar build agent before fanning out subagents
task_prompt:
Execute the dispatch step of the frame-debug sequence. The following frames were produced by step 1. Follow the dispatch protocol from `bar sequence show frame-debug` exactly.

## Frame 1: Database Connection Management Layer
Connection pool lifecycle, pool saturation, and connection allocation behavior in the order service.

## Frame 2: Request Timeout and Routing Layer
Timeout initiation in the checkout service, request queuing delays, and timing relationship between timeouts and backend resource state.
eval_gate: Before the dispatch step fans out to subagents, the transcript must contain a bar build tool call result whose output contains '=== TASK 任務 (DO THIS) ===' appearing before the first Agent tool call at this dispatch step. The bar build output is used to derive the agent configuration — the LLM reads it and writes agent prompts informed by it; verbatim inclusion is not required or expected. Absence of the bar build tool result before the first Agent spawn is a Criterion G failure.
notes: Tests Criterion G: pre-dispatch agent config. The orchestrator must synthesize a task-specific bar build agent command, produce a system prompt, and inject a verbatim excerpt of that output into each spawned Agent tool call. Without the pre-dispatch agent config protocol in bar-workflow skill guidance, agents skip directly from frame enumeration to subagent launch with no custom system prompt. The fix adds guidance to bar-workflow: 'Before executing any dispatch step, run bar build [task-appropriate tokens] agent and include a verbatim excerpt of the output in the prompt of each spawned subagent.' Two-part failure mode: (1) bar build agent not run at all; (2) bar build agent run but output not included in Agent tool call — both are Criterion G failures.
